summary: Iteration 5 backend + frontend testing complete. All 16 pytest cases (test_iteration5.py) PASSED covering: depot permission gating (admin & Chef de dépôt allowed, Vendeur 403 'Permission Dépôt requise'), Commande send from depot order to shop, list-filtered-by-shop_id data isolation, notification_message field stripped for non-depot users but present for admin, resolve-conforme adds articles to stock (auto-code + shop_id match), resolve-non-conforme with items+description, double-resolve returns 400, and permission migration ensures every user has 'permissions.depot' key. UI verified end-to-end at 1920x1080: (1) admin sidebar shows both Dépôt and Commande links; (2) Vendeur A (Boutique Centrale, PIN 111111) has NO Dépôt link but sees Commande; direct /depot navigation redirects to /; (3) Vendeur A sees 3 own commandes without notification banner; (4) Vendeur B (sogo, PIN 222222) sees 0 initially — data isolation confirmed via UI; (5) admin Dépôt order detail → ACTIONS → 'Envoyer à une boutique' opens dialog with shop select listing boutique-type shops only (Boutique Centrale + sogo), confirm sends and success toast appears; (6) after send, admin /commandes shows 4 cards including new 'sogo' one with 'En attente de validation' badge; (7) Vendeur B then sees the 1 new commande, opens detail, both action buttons present, non-conforme dialog with 180 line checkboxes + description textarea works, submit triggers 'Non-conformité signalée' toast and 'Commande non conforme' resolution summary; (8) admin then sees 4 notification banners at top of /commandes including the new 'sogo a signalé un problème...' message; (9) admin Users page: Shield icon opens perms dialog and perm-depot checkbox is present as expected. Stock page confirms 180 articles present in boutique Centrale after conforme resolution. Zero critical issues found.
action_items: None — iteration 5 is production-ready. Optional (non-blocking) polish suggestions listed under critical_code_review_comments.
critical_code_review_comments: commande_routes.py serialize() correctly strips 'notification_message' via has_permission(user,'depot','access'). Since permissions.depot is a boolean in DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS, has_permission returns the bool directly — verified working.
resolve_conforme case-insensitive regex match on nom is correct; also filters by shop_id so cross-shop names don't collide. Good.
next_article_code() dedupe check inside resolve_conforme is nice (dup fallback). No integrity errors observed.

depot_routes.send_order requires depot permission (via require_depot), correctly gating send capability to depot-permission users only. Verified: Vendeur cannot even see the depot module UI to reach send.

ProtectedRoute.jsx passes hasPerm(user, requirePermission) as a 2-arg call — works because in AuthContext hasPerm treats a boolean module (like 'depot') by returning it as-is regardless of the action arg.
Sidebar Truck icon for Commande is visually distinct from Warehouse icon for Dépôt. No confusion.

Nit: CommandeDetailPage.jsx submitNonConforme only sends items[].line_id + description (per line), which matches the NonConformeItem model. However the resolution-summary text on detail page only shows commande.resolution_note (the free-text field), not the per-line breakdown — acceptable for MVP but could be enhanced later.

Nit: on the /commandes list page, all 4 cards show 'CMD 56747' (same numero from same source depot order) — this is by design since one BL can be sent to multiple boutiques, but could be augmented visually with a distinct commande sequence number for clarity.
Nit: DialogContent aria-describedby warnings still present (persistent from prior iterations, purely cosmetic).

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all UI flows verified (send, isolation, conforme, non-conforme, notification gating, sidebar gating, route redirect)

test_credentials: Admin PIN 123456 (existing). Created via API in tests: A TEST_Vend (Vendeur, PIN 111111, Boutique Centrale), B TEST_Vend (Vendeur, PIN 222222, sogo), D TEST_Chef (Chef de dépôt, PIN 333333, Dépôt Central).
seed_data_creation: test_iteration5.py fixtures create/reset TEST_ prefixed users and ensure at least 2 boutiques. Sent multiple commandes from an existing depot order (CMD 56747) — one resolved conforme (adds 180 articles to Boutique Centrale stock, some already existed and incremented), one resolved non-conforme (line 1 flagged), plus one sent to 'sogo' via UI and resolved non-conforme via UI. These records remain in DB but are inert (statuses conforme/non_conforme). Main agent may leave them or clean up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 iterations are green. This iteration validates the Depot permission + Commande workflow. If iterating further: perm-depot checkbox in UsersPage is under a hardcoded MODULES=['reprise','devis','intervention','stock'] block with Caisse/Communication/Dépôt appended individually — if new module perms are added, follow the same pattern. Send/resolve endpoints use ObjectId conversion; any change to id format must update both sides. The startup migration in server.py backfills permissions.depot on existing users based on grade templates (idempotent, safe to re-run).
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — all specified acceptance criteria in the feature list were verified working end-to-end.
